type
organization
location_lat
-33.9
background_section
## Background Regen Sydney works in the Sydney Basin Bioregion on the lands of the Gadigal people. Like many wealthy cities, Sydney overshoots ecological limits while still falling short on social foundations—the “doughnut” gap between planetary boundaries and fair social outcomes. The bioregion mixes neighborhoods, catchments such as the Cooks River, and the wider metro area. Regen Sydney treats bioregions as open and changing—especially under climate stress—and edges between bioregions as seams of connection, not walls. Everyday rhythms—tides, seasonal bird movements, trees turning—offer handles for reconnection at neighborhood scale. ### Origins and Network Regen Sydney takes a systemic view: help shift toward a life-affirming economy by linking people already pursuing that shift, and—across generations—move Sydney as city and bioregion into the doughnut’s safe space. It acts mainly as convenor: creating conditions for those conversations and engagements. The neighborhood scale matters because people can feel community and living systems there in concrete ways.
patterns
Doughnut_Assessment|Network_Weaving|Participatory_Planning_and_Strategies|Socioeconomic_Indicators|Political_Advocacy

Sydney, NSW 2000, Australia
place
Sydney, New South Wales, Australia
activities

description
Regen Sydney is taking a systemic view to help transition Sydney as a city and bioregion toward a life-affirming economy, moving from anthropocentrism to ecocentrism through neighborhood-scale programs and regenerative action.
id
regen-sydney
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy Regen Sydney works across three layers—neighborhood programs, thematic regenerative action, and deep economic code—toward civic infrastructure of interdependence. The shift they seek is from passive consumption to active citizenship: not a vote every few years but ongoing responsibility as custodians of place and community. Through that work they hope to surface agency, connect allies, grow a movement, and clarify complementary roles. The through-line remains: transition toward a life-affirming economy, linking people already on that path, and—over generations—moving Sydney as city and bioregion into the doughnut’s safe space, meeting social needs without breaching ecological limits.
website
https://youtu.be/ntsUgQDkts0
bioregional_context
Regen Sydney operates in the Sydney Basin Bioregion, on the lands of the Gadigal people. Sydney is a metropolitan city facing challenges of transgressing biophysical boundaries while not meeting social thresholds, needing to move into the safe space of the doughnut.
